name: Helsinki
summary: Helsinki is Finland's seaside capital, built on a rocky peninsula sprinkled with hundreds of little islands. It is famous for its white domed cathedral on Senate Square and a giant sea fortress you reach by ferry.
funFacts: Helsinki Cathedral has five bright green domes and 297 wide white steps that climb up from Senate Square right in the middle of the city.
The Suomenlinna sea fortress is spread across six rocky islands and is so important it was named a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1991.
Finland has around 3.2 million saunas for only 5.5 million people, so there is roughly one steamy sauna for every two Finns.
In June the sun in Helsinki barely sets, giving almost 19 hours of daylight and glowing 'white nights' instead of dark evenings.
The Sibelius Monument is made of 600 hollow steel pipes welded together, and the wind makes them whistle like a giant outdoor organ.
thingsToDo: Climb the wide white steps of Helsinki Cathedral on Senate Square for a view across the city.
Catch the short ferry from Market Square to Suomenlinna sea fortress to explore old tunnels and cannons.
Step inside Temppeliaukio Rock Church, a round chapel blasted straight into solid bedrock.
Spot the gold stars on Uspenski Cathedral, the biggest Orthodox church in Western Europe.
Touch the silver pipes of the Sibelius Monument in its leafy seaside park.
Wander Market Square by the harbour to taste salmon soup and watch the boats come in.

wildlife.name: Brown Bear
wildlife.description: Finland's national animal, a huge furry bear that roams the deep northern forests.

wildlife.name: Reindeer
wildlife.description: Antlered animal of Lapland that pulls sleds and is herded by the Sámi people.

wildlife.name: Saimaa Ringed Seal
wildlife.description: Rare freshwater seal with ring-shaped spots, found only in Lake Saimaa.

wildlife.name: Eurasian Eagle-Owl
wildlife.description: One of the world's biggest owls, with bright orange eyes and feathery ear tufts.

localWord.meaning: A friendly, casual way to say 'hi' in Finnish.

images.alt: The big white Helsinki Cathedral with green domes stands tall above Senate Square on a sunny day.
The red-brick Uspenski Cathedral with its golden onion domes shining in the sun.
A bird's-eye view of Suomenlinna sea fortress, a chain of green islands surrounded by blue Baltic water.
Inside the Rock Church, a round room carved into stone with a shiny copper ceiling overhead.
The Sibelius Monument, a sculpture of hundreds of silver steel pipes welded together in a Helsinki park.
Market Square in Helsinki, with stalls and people beside boats in the harbour.
Young brown bears in a Finnish forest near Kuusamo.
A reindeer with big antlers in the wide open landscape of Finnish Lapland.
A Saimaa ringed seal with ring-shaped spots resting on a rock in Finland's Lake Saimaa.
A Eurasian eagle-owl with bright orange eyes and feathery ear tufts.
